action.reasoning: Observation: The latest refinement regressed to 0.5724, while the best checkpoint remains replay_tuned.pt at 0.6063. Analysis: Recent changes around longer main training and altered replay balance likely overfit/plasticity-shifted away from the sweet spot. The stable recipe is clearly the weighted replay tuned configuration. A low-risk next step is not another broad retune, but a targeted improvement to representation quality while preserving the best hyperparameters: initialize from pretrained ImageNet ResNet18 weights instead of training from scratch, keeping the same continual learning loop and checkpoint/predict contract. This can improve sample efficiency and old/new class separability on CIFAR with minimal code risk. Decision: Update model.py to load torchvision pretrained resnet18 weights with CIFAR-friendly stem, keep train.py defaults aligned to the best tuned recipe/save path, run one moderate training, smoke-test predict, and submit eval.
action.command: python train.py --save_path checkpoints/pretrained_tuned.pt --epochs 4 --finetune_epochs 3 --memory_size 4000 --replay_boost 1.6 --finetune_replay_boost 2.4 --batch_size 128 --lr 0.03 && python predict.py --data_path tmp_test --checkpoint checkpoints/pretrained_tuned.pt --output tmp_pred.json
